data.text: The package already has some of the foundation pieces, so I’m verifying the exact gaps rather than duplicating what exists. Next I’m checking the current context helpers, sentinel declarations, and whether `Store` already matches the ctx-first interfaces.

I confirmed there are no existing ctx-first `Store` methods anywhere in `internal/bead`, so a straight compile-time assertion would fail as-is. I’m letting the package tests finish before deciding whether this bead is actually implementable in-scope or whether I need to return a no-change rationale with that incompatibility documented.
The package test run is still active; I’m polling it directly so I can see whether the current tree already passes before I edit anything.

The acceptance text is pointing to a real API migration, not just declarations. I’m measuring the internal call surface now to see if the ctx-first change is tractable in this bead without crossing into sibling event work.

I’m waiting on the package test run to settle before I decide between implementation and a documented block.

data.text: I’ve confirmed the blocker: satisfying the requested `Store` assertions would require a broad ctx-first API migration across `internal/bead`, not just the three small surfaces in the acceptance text. Because this bead is already at the decomposition depth cap, I’m recording that constraint as evidence instead of making a partial or inconsistent code change.

Blocked on scope, so I wrote [`.ddx/executions/20260512T194750-a0fb7c6f/no_changes_rationale.txt`](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-215b08a2-20260512T194750-a0fb7c6f/.ddx/executions/20260512T194750-a0fb7c6f/no_changes_rationale.txt) with `status: blocked`.

Reason: `backend.go` already declares the ctx-first foundation interfaces, but `store.go` still only exposes the legacy no-`ctx` methods, so the requested `*Store` compile-time assertions would require a broader API migration across `internal/bead` than this depth-3 bead can safely take on in one pass.

No commit was made.
